data.title: Wheaton Precious Metals buys gold stream from Montage Gold in $625M deal
data.description:
Wheaton Precious Metals (NYSE:WPM) +0.7% pre-market Thursday after it agreed to acquire a gold stream from Montage Gold's (OTCQX:MAUTF) Koné gold project in Côte d'Ivoire for $625M in cash upfront plus ongoing payments for gold ounces delivered equal to 20% of gold spot price.

The mine's attributable gold stream production is forecast to average more than 60K oz/year for the first five years, more than 47K oz/year for the first 10 years, and 34K oz/year for the life of mine; the project is forecast to have a 16-year mine life based on reserves, with first production anticipated in early 2027.

Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM) also will provide Montage (OTCQX:MAUTF) with a secured debt facility of up to $75M to be allocated to project costs, including cost overruns.

"Supported by strong shareholder backing from the Lundin Group and Zijin Mining, the Koné Project is expected to significantly boost Wheaton's near-term annual gold production and further strengthen our peer-leading growth trajectory," President and CEO Randy Smallwood said.

data.title: FTI Consulting GAAP EPS of $1.85 misses by $0.21, revenue of $926.02M misses by $20.14M
data.description:
• FTI Consulting press release (NYSE:FCN): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.85 misses by $0.21.
  • Revenue of $926.02M (+3.7% Y/Y) misses by $20.14M.
  • 2024 GuidanceThe Company is updating its full year 2024 guidance ranges for revenues and EPS. The Company now estimates that revenues for full year 2024 will range between $3.700 billion and $3.750 billion, which compares to the prior range of between $3.700 billion and $3.790 billion, vs. $3.76B consensus. The Company now estimates EPS for full year 2024 will range between $7.90 and $8.35, vs. $8.53 consensus, which compares to the prior range of between $8.10 and $8.60. The Company does not currently expect Adjusted EPS to differ from EPS.

data.title: American Airlines in charts: TRASM and PRASM decline Q/Q and Y/Y as cost increases
Beasley Broadcast names Coleman as new finance chief
data.description:
• Beasley Broadcast Group (NASDAQ:BBGI) said it has appointed Lauren Coleman as its CFO, effective, November 1.
  • The firm said its longtime finance chief Marie Tedesco will retire from the company after more than three decades of service to the firm.
  • Coleman served as Global Head of Strategic Corporate and Commercial Finance at Wayfair, before joining BBGI.
